topics.title: Information Security
topics.definition: Information Security (InfoSec) is the practice of protecting information and information systems from unauthorized access, disclosure, modification, destruction, or disruption. It protects both digital and physical information throughout its lifecycle.
examPoints: Information Security (InfoSec) is the practice of protecting information and information systems from unauthorized access, disclosure, modification, destruction, or disruption. It protects both digital and physical information throughout its lifecycle.
Protects Data, Systems, Networks, Applications, People, Processes
topics.narration:
Information Security

Information Security (InfoSec) is the practice of protecting information and information systems from unauthorized access, disclosure, modification, destruction, or disruption. It protects both digital and physical information throughout its lifecycle.

Important exam points. Information Security (InfoSec) is the practice of protecting information and information systems from unauthorized access, disclosure, modification, destruction, or disruption. It protects both digital and physical information throughout its lifecycle.. Goal: CIA Triad (Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability). Protects Data, Systems, Networks, Applications, People, Processes

topics.title: CIA Triad
topics.definition: The CIA Triad is the fundamental security model consisting of Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability. Every security control supports one or more of these principles.
examPoints: The CIA Triad is the fundamental security model consisting of Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability. Every security control supports one or more of these principles.
topics.narration:
CIA Triad

The CIA Triad is the fundamental security model consisting of Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability. Every security control supports one or more of these principles.

Important exam points. The CIA Triad is the fundamental security model consisting of Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability. Every security control supports one or more of these principles.. Confidentiality → Encryption, Access Control. Integrity → Hashing, Digital Signatures. Availability → Backup, Redundancy, Load Balancing

topics.title: Confidentiality
topics.definition: Confidentiality ensures that information is accessible only to authorized users and prevents unauthorized disclosure.
examPoints: Confidentiality ensures that information is accessible only to authorized users and prevents unauthorized disclosure.
Achieved using Encryption, VPN, Access Control
Example: Bank account details visible only to the account owner
topics.narration:
Confidentiality

Confidentiality ensures that information is accessible only to authorized users and prevents unauthorized disclosure.

Important exam points. Confidentiality ensures that information is accessible only to authorized users and prevents unauthorized disclosure.. Achieved using Encryption, VPN, Access Control. Example: Bank account details visible only to the account owner

topics.title: Integrity
topics.definition: Integrity ensures that information remains accurate, complete, and unaltered except by authorized users.
examPoints: Integrity ensures that information remains accurate, complete, and unaltered except by authorized users.
Achieved using Hashing, Checksums, Digital Signatures
Example: Downloaded software has not been modified
topics.narration:
Integrity

Integrity ensures that information remains accurate, complete, and unaltered except by authorized users.

Important exam points. Integrity ensures that information remains accurate, complete, and unaltered except by authorized users.. Achieved using Hashing, Checksums, Digital Signatures. Example: Downloaded software has not been modified

topics.title: Availability
topics.definition: Availability ensures that systems and information remain accessible whenever authorized users require them.
examPoints: Availability ensures that systems and information remain accessible whenever authorized users require them.
Achieved using Backups, RAID, Clustering, UPS, Failover
topics.narration:
Availability

Availability ensures that systems and information remain accessible whenever authorized users require them.

Important exam points. Availability ensures that systems and information remain accessible whenever authorized users require them.. Achieved using Backups, RAID, Clustering, UPS, Failover. DoS attacks mainly affect Availability

topics.title: AAA
topics.definition: AAA stands for Authentication, Authorization, and Accounting (Accountability). It controls user identity, permissions, and activity logging.
examPoints: AAA stands for Authentication, Authorization, and Accounting (Accountability). It controls user identity, permissions, and activity logging.
topics.narration:
AAA

AAA stands for Authentication, Authorization, and Accounting (Accountability). It controls user identity, permissions, and activity logging.

Important exam points. AAA stands for Authentication, Authorization, and Accounting (Accountability). It controls user identity, permissions, and activity logging.. Authentication → Who are you?. Authorization → What can you access?. Accounting → What did you do?

topics.title: Authentication
topics.definition: Authentication is the process of verifying the identity of a user, device, or application before granting access.
examPoints: Authentication is the process of verifying the identity of a user, device, or application before granting access.
Something you have → Smart Card, OTP
Something you are → Fingerprint, Face ID
topics.narration:
Authentication

Authentication is the process of verifying the identity of a user, device, or application before granting access.

Important exam points. Authentication is the process of verifying the identity of a user, device, or application before granting access.. Authentication Factors:. Something you know → Password. Something you have → Smart Card, OTP. Something you are → Fingerprint, Face ID

topics.title: Authorization
topics.definition: Authorization determines what authenticated users are permitted to access or perform.
examPoints: Authorization determines what authenticated users are permitted to access or perform.
Example: Admin can delete users, normal user cannot
topics.narration:
Authorization

Authorization determines what authenticated users are permitted to access or perform.

Important exam points. Authorization determines what authenticated users are permitted to access or perform.. Happens after Authentication. Example: Admin can delete users, normal user cannot

topics.title: Accountability (Accounting)
topics.definition: Accountability ensures every action performed by a user can be traced back to that user using logs and audit trails.
examPoints: Accountability ensures every action performed by a user can be traced back to that user using logs and audit trails.
topics.narration:
Accountability (Accounting)

Accountability ensures every action performed by a user can be traced back to that user using logs and audit trails.

Important exam points. Accountability ensures every action performed by a user can be traced back to that user using logs and audit trails.. Achieved through Audit Logs. Important for Compliance and Forensics

topics.title: Non-Repudiation
topics.definition: Non-repudiation ensures that the sender cannot deny sending a message and the receiver cannot deny receiving it.
examPoints: Non-repudiation ensures that the sender cannot deny sending a message and the receiver cannot deny receiving it.
Hash alone does not provide Non-Repudiation
topics.narration:
Non-Repudiation

Non-repudiation ensures that the sender cannot deny sending a message and the receiver cannot deny receiving it.

Important exam points. Non-repudiation ensures that the sender cannot deny sending a message and the receiver cannot deny receiving it.. Achieved using Digital Signatures. Hash alone does not provide Non-Repudiation

topics.title: Privacy
topics.definition: Privacy ensures that personal or sensitive information is collected, processed, and shared only according to legal and organizational policies.
examPoints: Privacy ensures that personal or sensitive information is collected, processed, and shared only according to legal and organizational policies.
topics.narration:
Privacy

Privacy ensures that personal or sensitive information is collected, processed, and shared only according to legal and organizational policies.

Important exam points. Privacy ensures that personal or sensitive information is collected, processed, and shared only according to legal and organizational policies.. Privacy protects personal data.. Confidentiality protects all sensitive information.

topics.title: Threat
topics.definition: A threat is anything that has the potential to exploit a vulnerability and cause damage.
examPoints: A threat is anything that has the potential to exploit a vulnerability and cause damage.
topics.narration:
Threat

A threat is anything that has the potential to exploit a vulnerability and cause damage.

Important exam points. A threat is anything that has the potential to exploit a vulnerability and cause damage.. Examples:. Hacker. Malware. Fire. Flood. Insider

topics.title: Vulnerability
topics.definition: A vulnerability is a weakness in software, hardware, people, or processes that can be exploited.
examPoints: A vulnerability is a weakness in software, hardware, people, or processes that can be exploited.
topics.narration:
Vulnerability

A vulnerability is a weakness in software, hardware, people, or processes that can be exploited.

Important exam points. A vulnerability is a weakness in software, hardware, people, or processes that can be exploited.. Examples:. Weak Password. Unpatched Software. Open Port

topics.title: Attack
topics.definition: An attack is the actual attempt to exploit a vulnerability using a threat.
examPoints: An attack is the actual attempt to exploit a vulnerability using a threat.
topics.narration:
Attack

An attack is the actual attempt to exploit a vulnerability using a threat.

Important exam points. An attack is the actual attempt to exploit a vulnerability using a threat.. Examples:. SQL Injection. DDoS. MITM. Brute Force

topics.title: Risk
topics.definition: Risk is the possibility that a threat will successfully exploit a vulnerability and cause damage.
examPoints: Risk is the possibility that a threat will successfully exploit a vulnerability and cause damage.
topics.narration:
Risk

Risk is the possibility that a threat will successfully exploit a vulnerability and cause damage.

Important exam points. Risk is the possibility that a threat will successfully exploit a vulnerability and cause damage.. Risk = Threat × Vulnerability × Impact. Higher Impact = Higher Risk

topics.title: Threat vs Vulnerability vs Attack vs Risk
comparisons: Term: Risk; Meaning: Probability of loss
topics.narration:
Threat vs Vulnerability vs Attack vs Risk

Important exam points. Vulnerability → Can be attacked. Attack → Is attacking. Risk → Expected damage

Comparison. Term: Threat; Meaning: Potential danger. Term: Vulnerability; Meaning: Weakness. Term: Attack; Meaning: Actual exploitation. Term: Risk; Meaning: Probability of loss

topics.title: Security Goals
topics.definition: Security goals define the objectives that information security aims to achieve.
examPoints: Security goals define the objectives that information security aims to achieve.
topics.narration:
Security Goals

Security goals define the objectives that information security aims to achieve.

Important exam points. Security goals define the objectives that information security aims to achieve.. Remember:. CIA. Authentication. Authorization. Accountability. Non-Repudiation. Privacy

topics.title: Security Services
topics.definition: Security services are mechanisms that implement security goals within a system.
examPoints: Security services are mechanisms that implement security goals within a system.
topics.narration:
Security Services

Security services are mechanisms that implement security goals within a system.

Important exam points. Security services are mechanisms that implement security goals within a system.. Examples:. Authentication. Confidentiality. Integrity. Access Control. Non-Repudiation. Availability

topics.title: Passive Attack
topics.definition: A passive attack monitors or intercepts communication without modifying data.
examPoints: A passive attack monitors or intercepts communication without modifying data.
topics.narration:
Passive Attack

A passive attack monitors or intercepts communication without modifying data.

Important exam points. A passive attack monitors or intercepts communication without modifying data.. Examples:. Packet Sniffing. Traffic Analysis. Hard to detect

topics.title: Active Attack
topics.definition: An active attack modifies, injects, deletes, or disrupts data or services.
examPoints: An active attack modifies, injects, deletes, or disrupts data or services.
Easy to detect, harder to prevent
topics.narration:
Active Attack

An active attack modifies, injects, deletes, or disrupts data or services.

Important exam points. An active attack modifies, injects, deletes, or disrupts data or services.. Examples:. MITM. Replay. DoS. Data Modification. Easy to detect, harder to prevent

topics.title: Passive vs Active Attack
comparisons: Passive: Reads Data; Active: Modifies Data
Passive: Hard to Detect; Active: Easier to Detect
topics.narration:
Passive vs Active Attack

Comparison. Passive: Reads Data; Active: Modifies Data. Passive: No Modification; Active: Modification. Passive: Hard to Detect; Active: Easier to Detect. Passive: Confidentiality; Active: Integrity & Availability

topics.title: Malware
topics.definition: Malware is malicious software designed to damage systems, steal information, or gain unauthorized access.
examPoints: Malware is malicious software designed to damage systems, steal information, or gain unauthorized access.
topics.narration:
Malware

Malware is malicious software designed to damage systems, steal information, or gain unauthorized access.

Important exam points. Malware is malicious software designed to damage systems, steal information, or gain unauthorized access.. Types:. Virus. Worm. Trojan. Spyware. Ransomware. Rootkit

topics.title: Social Engineering
topics.definition: Social engineering manipulates people into revealing confidential information or performing insecure actions.
examPoints: Social engineering manipulates people into revealing confidential information or performing insecure actions.
topics.narration:
Social Engineering

Social engineering manipulates people into revealing confidential information or performing insecure actions.

Important exam points. Social engineering manipulates people into revealing confidential information or performing insecure actions.. Examples:. Phishing. Vishing. Smishing. Tailgating. Shoulder Surfing

topics.title: Insider Threat
topics.definition: An insider threat originates from authorized users such as employees, contractors, or administrators.
examPoints: An insider threat originates from authorized users such as employees, contractors, or administrators.
topics.narration:
Insider Threat

An insider threat originates from authorized users such as employees, contractors, or administrators.

Important exam points. An insider threat originates from authorized users such as employees, contractors, or administrators.. Types:. Malicious Insider. Negligent Insider

topics.title: Cryptography
topics.definition: Cryptography is the science of securing information by converting plaintext into ciphertext using mathematical algorithms and cryptographic keys.
examPoints: Cryptography is the science of securing information by converting plaintext into ciphertext using mathematical algorithms and cryptographic keys.
topics.narration:
Cryptography

Cryptography is the science of securing information by converting plaintext into ciphertext using mathematical algorithms and cryptographic keys.

Important exam points. Cryptography is the science of securing information by converting plaintext into ciphertext using mathematical algorithms and cryptographic keys.. Goals:. Confidentiality. Integrity. Authentication. Non-Repudiation

topics.title: Plaintext
topics.definition: Original readable information before encryption.
topics.narration:
Plaintext

Original readable information before encryption.

Important exam points. Original readable information before encryption.. Input to Encryption

topics.title: Ciphertext
topics.definition: Encrypted unreadable data produced after encryption.
examPoints: Encrypted unreadable data produced after encryption.
topics.narration:
Ciphertext

Encrypted unreadable data produced after encryption.

Important exam points. Encrypted unreadable data produced after encryption.. Output of Encryption

topics.title: Encryption
topics.definition: Encryption converts plaintext into ciphertext using an algorithm and key.
examPoints: Encryption converts plaintext into ciphertext using an algorithm and key.
topics.narration:
Encryption

Encryption converts plaintext into ciphertext using an algorithm and key.

Important exam points. Encryption converts plaintext into ciphertext using an algorithm and key.. Confidentiality

topics.title: Decryption
topics.definition: Decryption converts ciphertext back into plaintext using the correct key.
examPoints: Decryption converts ciphertext back into plaintext using the correct key.
topics.narration:
Decryption

Decryption converts ciphertext back into plaintext using the correct key.

Important exam points. Decryption converts ciphertext back into plaintext using the correct key.. Reverse process of Encryption

topics.title: Symmetric Cryptography
topics.definition: Uses the same key for both encryption and decryption.
examPoints: Uses the same key for both encryption and decryption.
topics.narration:
Symmetric Cryptography

Uses the same key for both encryption and decryption.

Important exam points. Uses the same key for both encryption and decryption.. Examples:. AES. DES. RC4. Fast

topics.title: Asymmetric Cryptography
topics.definition: Uses a Public Key for encryption/verification and a Private Key for decryption/signing.
examPoints: Uses a Public Key for encryption/verification and a Private Key for decryption/signing.
Slow but secure for key exchange
topics.narration:
Asymmetric Cryptography

Uses a Public Key for encryption/verification and a Private Key for decryption/signing.

Important exam points. Uses a Public Key for encryption/verification and a Private Key for decryption/signing.. Examples:. RSA. ECC. Slow but secure for key exchange

topics.title: Hashing
topics.definition: Hashing converts data into a fixed-length hash value and is a one-way function.
examPoints: Hashing converts data into a fixed-length hash value and is a one-way function.
topics.narration:
Hashing

Hashing converts data into a fixed-length hash value and is a one-way function.

Important exam points. Hashing converts data into a fixed-length hash value and is a one-way function.. No Decryption. Used for Integrity. SHA-256. SHA-512

topics.title: Digital Signature
topics.definition: A digital signature uses the sender's private key to sign data and the public key to verify it.
examPoints: A digital signature uses the sender's private key to sign data and the public key to verify it.
topics.narration:
Digital Signature

A digital signature uses the sender's private key to sign data and the public key to verify it.

Important exam points. A digital signature uses the sender's private key to sign data and the public key to verify it.. Authentication. Integrity. Non-Repudiation

topics.title: Real-world Applications
comparisons: Technology: HTTPS; Security Used: TLS + PKI
Technology: WhatsApp; Security Used: End-to-End Encryption
Technology: Email; Security Used: Digital Signature
Technology: Software Updates; Security Used: Code Signing
topics.narration:
Real-world Applications

Comparison. Technology: HTTPS; Security Used: TLS + PKI. Technology: VPN; Security Used: Encryption. Technology: Banking; Security Used: PKI. Technology: WhatsApp; Security Used: End-to-End Encryption. Technology: Email; Security Used: Digital Signature. Technology: Software Updates; Security Used: Code Signing

topics.title: Top 20 One-Liners for Exam
oneLiners: Digital Signature provides Authentication + Integrity + Non-Repudiation.
Passive attack reads; Active attack modifies.
PKI is built on the principles introduced in this phase: CIA, authentication, cryptography, and trust.
topics.narration:
Top 20 One-Liners for Exam

Memory points. CIA = Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability.. Encryption provides Confidentiality.. Hashing provides Integrity.. Digital Signature provides Authentication + Integrity + Non-Repudiation.. Authentication comes before Authorization.. Authorization decides permissions.. Accountability uses audit logs.. Threat exploits Vulnerability.. Attack is the actual exploitation.. Risk = Threat × Vulnerability × Impact.. Passive attack reads; Active attack modifies.. DoS attacks Availability.. Sniffing is a Passive attack.. MITM is an Active attack.. Symmetric uses one key.. Asymmetric uses two keys.. Plaintext → Encryption → Ciphertext.. Ciphertext → Decryption → Plaintext.. Hashing is one-way.. PKI is built on the principles introduced in this phase: CIA, authentication, cryptography, and trust.
